mcqs.question: What is the root of a tree in data structures?

options.text: The node with the maximum number of children
The topmost node that has no parent
The leftmost node in the tree
The node that was inserted first

mcqs.explanation: The root is the topmost node in a tree hierarchy and is the only node that has no parent. All other nodes in the tree are descendants of the root. The root is unique in a tree and serves as the entry point for tree traversals.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of a tree data structure?

options.text: A tree must have exactly one root node
Every node except the root has exactly one parent
Trees can contain cycles
A tree with n nodes has exactly (n-1) edges

mcqs.explanation: Trees cannot contain cycles. This is a fundamental property that distinguishes trees from graphs. If there is a cycle, the structure becomes a graph, not a tree. The other options are all correct properties of trees.

mcqs.question: A node that has no children is called:

options.text: Parent node
Internal node
Root node
Leaf node

mcqs.explanation: A leaf node (also called an external node) is a node with no children. It is the terminal node in a tree hierarchy. In the example tree with root A, nodes D, G, E, and F would be leaf nodes.

mcqs.question: In a tree, the degree of a node refers to:

options.text: The length of the longest path from the node to a leaf
The number of children a node has
The depth of the node from the root
The number of ancestors the node has

mcqs.explanation: The degree of a node is defined as the number of children it has. For example, if a node has 3 children, its degree is 3. A leaf node has degree 0. This is different from the degree in graph theory.

mcqs.question: What is the depth of the root node in any tree?

options.text: Equal to the height of the tree
Undefined

mcqs.explanation: The depth of the root node is always 0. Depth is measured as the number of edges from the root to the node. Since the root has no parent, it is at depth 0. Some texts may use depth starting from 1, but the convention in data structures is 0 for root.

mcqs.question: In a tree with n nodes, what is the minimum possible height?

options.text: n
⌊log₂n⌋

mcqs.explanation: The minimum height of a tree with n nodes is achieved when the tree is as balanced as possible. For a binary tree, this minimum height is ⌊log₂n⌋. A completely balanced binary tree has height approximately log₂n, while a completely skewed tree (like a linked list) has height n-1.

mcqs.question: The height of a tree is defined as:

options.text: The maximum level number in the tree
The maximum number of edges from the root to any leaf
The total number of nodes in the tree
The number of root-to-leaf paths

mcqs.explanation: The height of a tree is the maximum depth (or maximum number of edges) from the root to any leaf node. If a tree has only a root node, its height is 0. This is equivalent to the longest path from root to leaf, counting edges not nodes.

mcqs.question: Consider a tree where node X is the parent of node Y. Then node Y is called:

options.text: Ancestor of X
Sibling of X
Child of X
Subtree of X

mcqs.explanation: In a tree hierarchy, if X is the parent of Y, then Y is the child of X. This establishes the parent-child relationship. Ancestor refers to nodes above in the hierarchy, siblings share the same parent, and subtree refers to the entire structure starting from a node.

mcqs.question: What is the maximum number of nodes at level L in a binary tree?

options.text: L
L²
2ᴸ
2ᴸ⁻¹

mcqs.explanation: In a binary tree, level 0 can have 1 node (root), level 1 can have up to 2 nodes, level 2 can have up to 4 nodes, and so on. The maximum number of nodes at level L is 2ᴸ. This follows from the property that each node can have at most 2 children.

mcqs.question: A subtree in a tree is defined as:

options.text: Any leaf node in the tree
A node and all its descendants
The root node and its children only
Any path from root to leaf

mcqs.explanation: A subtree consists of a node (called the root of the subtree) and all its descendants (children, grandchildren, and so on). Every node in a tree is the root of some subtree. This recursive definition is fundamental to tree operations and traversals.

mcqs.question: In a binary tree, what is the maximum possible degree of any node?

options.text: Unlimited

mcqs.explanation: In a binary tree, each node can have at most 2 children (commonly referred to as left child and right child). Therefore, the maximum degree of any node in a binary tree is 2. This is what distinguishes binary trees from general trees.

mcqs.question: Which of the following statements is TRUE about trees?

options.text: A tree with 10 nodes must have exactly 10 edges
Every node in a tree has exactly one parent except the root
The height of a tree is always equal to its number of levels
Leaf nodes have degree 1

mcqs.explanation: In a tree data structure, every node except the root has exactly one parent. This is a defining characteristic of trees. Option A is false (n nodes have n-1 edges), option C is false (height = levels - 1), and option D is false (leaf nodes have degree 0).

mcqs.question: The relationship between siblings in a tree is that they:

options.text: Have the same parent
Are at the same level
Have the same depth from root
All of the above

mcqs.explanation: Siblings are nodes that share the same parent. Since they are children of the same parent, they are necessarily at the same level and same depth from the root. Therefore, all three statements are true about siblings.

mcqs.question: What is the total number of edges in a tree with 25 nodes?

options.text: Cannot be determined

mcqs.explanation: A fundamental property of trees is that a tree with n nodes always has exactly (n-1) edges. This can be proven by considering that starting from the root, each new node adds exactly one edge to connect it to the existing tree. Thus, 25 nodes require 24 edges.

mcqs.question: In a tree, an ancestor of a node is:

options.text: Any node on the path from the node to the root
Any node that is a child of the node
Any node at the same level as the node
Any node with the same parent as the node

mcqs.explanation: An ancestor of a node is any node that appears on the path from that node up to the root. This includes the parent, grandparent, great-grandparent, and so on, all the way to the root. The node itself is also considered an ancestor of itself in some definitions.

mcqs.question: A full binary tree (or proper binary tree) is one in which:

options.text: Every node has exactly 0 or 2 children
Every node has either 0 or 2 children (no node has exactly 1 child)
All levels except possibly the last are completely filled
All leaf nodes are at the same level

mcqs.explanation: A full binary tree (also called a proper or strict binary tree) is defined as a binary tree where every node has either 0 or 2 children - no node has exactly 1 child. This is different from a complete binary tree where all levels except possibly the last are filled.

mcqs.question: If the height of a binary tree is h, what is the maximum number of nodes it can contain?

options.text: 2ʰ
h²

mcqs.explanation: A binary tree of height h can have at most 2ʰ⁺¹ - 1 nodes. This occurs when the tree is a perfect binary tree (all levels are completely filled). For example, a tree with height 2 can have at most 2³ - 1 = 7 nodes. This is derived from the geometric series: 1 + 2 + 4 + ... + 2ʰ.

mcqs.question: In a tree, if node A is at level 3 and node B is at level 5, what can be said about their relationship?

options.text: A must be an ancestor of B
B must be a descendant of A
A cannot be a descendant of B
They must be in the same subtree

mcqs.explanation: Since A is at level 3 (closer to root) and B is at level 5 (farther from root), A cannot be a descendant of B. However, they may or may not be related - they could be in different branches of the tree. Being at different levels doesn't establish a direct ancestor-descendant relationship.

mcqs.question: What is the minimum number of nodes in a binary tree of height h?

options.text: h
2ʰ
h²

mcqs.explanation: The minimum number of nodes in a binary tree of height h is h + 1. This occurs when the tree is completely skewed (like a linked list), where each node has only one child. The height is measured in edges, so a tree with just one node has height 0 and minimum nodes = 0 + 1 = 1.

mcqs.question: A complete binary tree of n nodes, when stored in an array, has the property that:

options.text: The root is at index 0, and for any node at index i, its left child is at 2i+1
The root is at index 1, and for any node at index i, its left child is at 2i
The tree must be perfectly balanced
Only leaf nodes are stored in the array

mcqs.explanation: When a complete binary tree is stored in an array (level-order traversal), if we use 1-based indexing, the parent of node at index i is at ⌊i/2⌋, the left child is at 2i, and the right child is at 2i+1. This is the standard array representation for heap data structures.
